mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate at 3.9% is historically low, indicating a tight labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If UNRATE remains below 4% but forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 60% of such instances, or rank IC < 0.02 over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) at 700,727 million dollars is the highest on record, suggesting robust consumer demand that boosts corporate revenues and equity prices.
failure_condition: If within 5 trading days after RSAFS exceeds 700,000 the S&P 500 declines in more than 70% of cases, or the average return is negative.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 5.33% is elevated, signaling a restrictive monetary stance that slows economic activity and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: If after a trigger, forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of cases, or the average return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) reflect broad-based demand strength across the economy, which supports corporate earnings and drives equity markets higher.
failure_condition: If both HOUST and RSAFS rise compared to their previous values but the S&P 500 forward 20-day return is negative in more than half of such periods, or the Pearson correlation between the joint signal and returns is below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) exceeds 310, high inflation pressures the central bank to tighten policy, and a high federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) confirms this, leading to lower equity valuations.
failure_condition: If after a trigger, the joint increase in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL is associated with positive returns in more than 30% of cases, or the Rank IC over a 3-year window is below -0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 102.33 indicates that the manufacturing sector is expanding, which typically signals improving business investment and higher corporate earnings.
failure_condition: If weekly forward returns following any INDPRO release are negative in over 50% of cases, or the average return is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 157,000 thousand jobs signals strong job creation, which boosts consumer confidence and spending, positively impacting stock prices.
failure_condition: If after a trigger, the forward 60-day return is negative more than half the time, or the correlation between PAYEMS level and returns is zero or negative.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP (GDPC1) is above 22,500 billion dollars and housing starts (HOUST) exceed 1,300 thousand units, the economy shows sustained demand from both investment and consumption, supporting a rally in equities.
failure_condition: If both conditions hold but forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 40% of instances, or the joint signal fails to produce a Sharpe ratio above 0.5 over any 2-year period.
